content:
{
  "title": "Counterfactual Role-Reversal Prompting: Mitigating Social Biases in LLM Outputs via Perspective Inversion",
  "problem": "Large language models (LLMs) persistently exhibit social biases and stereotypes in generated text, even under bias-mitigation prompting strategies. Existing methods insufficiently address subtle and intersectional biases without retraining or significant architectural changes.",
  "motivation": "As LLMs are rapidly integrated into sensitive applications, robust and generalizable bias-reduction strategies at inference-time are urgently needed. Prompt-based debiasing that can uncover and neutralize both explicit and implicit stereotypes, especially in complex, multi-attribute contexts, remains a critical gap. A method that dynamically exposes the model to counterfactual perspectives may surface and counteract ingrained biases.",
  "method": "We propose 'Counterfactual Role-Reversal Prompting' (CRRP), a prompting technique that generates model outputs for a given input in both the original and an attribute-reversed scenario (e.g., swapping gender and/or ethnicity roles in contextually appropriate ways). The final output is produced by explicit contrastive reconciliation—merging or interpolating between the two responses using a meta-prompt that instructs the model to identify and neutralize bias-inducing differences. This method requires no model retraining and operates entirely via prompt engineering, and can be applied to any sensitive attribute (e.g., age, gender, race, religion).",
  "experiment_plan": "We will evaluate CRRP on benchmark datasets for social bias in LLMs such as CrowS-Pairs, StereoSet, and BBQ. Baselines will include standard, self-debiasing, and chain-of-thought prompting approaches. Metrics will include automatic bias scores (e.g., stereotyping, association), human evaluations of fairness and utility, and ablation studies across attributes and domains."
}
